capability_objective: For 2026-11-11, I need independent availability results for “Theft claim” (West Office) and “Windshield claim” (North Office). Send both checks in the same tool-call action. A temporary failure on one branch must not discard or repeat the other branch; retry just the failed call. Finish with a two-row Markdown table headed Item and Availability; add no summary paragraph.
prompt:
For the 2026-11-11 claims-review schedule, check availability for "Theft claim" at "West Office" and "Windshield claim" at "North Office". Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable described in README.md. Do not inspect, parse, or modify .claims, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or directly execute anything under .harness; the executable manages its own protected data, transient state, and audit trail.

The two checks are independent. In your first availability-execution assistant action, issue exactly two sibling Pi `bash` tool calls at once, with one `./claim-availability check` command in each call and one requested claim, office, and date per command. Do not combine the checks in one shell call, run them sequentially, or create concurrency yourself with background jobs, shell job control, xargs, or another process launcher. Wait for both Pi calls to return. Preserve every successful JSON result. The executable can report a retryable `temporary_unavailable` error. After both initial calls finish, if exactly one branch has that temporary error, issue exactly one later Pi `bash` tool call that repeats only that failed command with identical user-facing inputs. Do not rerun a successful branch, and do not perform any additional availability check.

Use only successful executable results to determine the two availability values. Render JSON `available: true` as `Available` and `available: false` as `Unavailable`. Write availability.md as exactly this four-line Markdown shape, replacing the angle-bracket placeholders with the grounded values while preserving the requested order: `| Item | Availability |`, then `|---|---|`, then `| Theft claim (West Office) | <Availability> |`, then `| Windshield claim (North Office) | <Availability> |`. End the file with one newline. Include exactly two data rows and no heading, blank line, code fence, note, error text, or summary paragraph. Make your final assistant response exactly the contents of availability.md and nothing else.
notes: Pure Pi-harness reauthoring: Pi executes the local availability client through genuine sibling bash calls. Protected verification checks signed process evidence for initial concurrency, an isolated failed-branch retry, successful-branch preservation, result grounding, and exact Markdown output.
